verdict: UNHEDGED Sharpe -0.79 (vs HEDGED +0.62); 153% of unhedged cumPnL is the MARKET/beta part (idiosyncratic adds the same sub-cost ≈+0.62 either way). The unhedged 'edge' is ≈ the zero-skill passive-net-beta benchmark (-1.15) ⇒ NOT skill — it is uncompensated, drifting market exposure = this sample's BTC drift, which inverts in a down market (maxDD -9168 bps). Not hedging does NOT rescue the strategy: it adds undirected market risk to an already-sub-cost idiosyncratic signal. 'Hold market risk' only makes sense as a separate, explicit beta-allocation decision (then you'd just hold the index — the model is irrelevant and cannot time it: Steps 99/100/101). Production LGBM unaffected.
